meaning: Replace hybrid battery pack / battery deterioration detected.
likely_causes: One or more battery blocks are weak or imbalanced
Battery cooling fan or duct is restricted
High mileage or repeated heat exposure accelerated module aging
low_risk_checks: Verify the 12V battery is healthy before deeper hybrid diagnosis
Inspect battery cooling intake, fan, and ducts for lint, pet hair, or blockage
Review block-voltage and state-of-charge imbalance with a hybrid-capable scan tool
escalation: Escalate if confirmation would require pack opening, live HV measurement, or module replacement.

meaning: Hybrid battery pack deterioration / loss of usable capacity.
likely_causes: Aged battery modules with reduced capacity
Repeated high-temperature operation
Battery cooling airflow issue
low_risk_checks: Confirm customer complaint matches rapid state-of-charge swings or poor EV assist
Inspect cooling intake and fan operation
Compare live-data block voltages and temperature sensor readings
escalation: Escalate if service requires internal battery inspection or module balancing.

meaning: Inverter cooling system performance problem causing inverter or converter overheating risk.
likely_causes: Inverter coolant pump failure
Low coolant level or trapped air
Restricted heat exchanger or poor coolant flow
low_risk_checks: Check inverter coolant reservoir level only when safe and cold
Look for visible turbulence or flow in the inverter reservoir where applicable
Inspect related pump fuses, relay supply, and obvious leaks
escalation: Escalate if diagnosis requires energized HV testing, inverter disassembly, or coolant bleed procedures beyond service information.

meaning: DC/DC converter or inverter performance fault affecting propulsion or READY-state behavior.
likely_causes: Internal inverter or converter fault
Low-voltage supply issue causing control instability
Companion cooling problem leading to protective shutdown
low_risk_checks: Verify 12V battery health and module supply voltage first
Check for companion inverter cooling faults such as P0A93
Inspect related low-voltage fuses, relays, and obvious coolant leaks
escalation: Escalate if the next step would require inverter disassembly, energized HV testing, or internal converter diagnosis.

meaning: Battery management system faults related to pack protection, isolation, or thermal limits.
likely_causes: Pack isolation concern or moisture intrusion
Cell imbalance or sensor disagreement
Battery temperature outside normal operating window
low_risk_checks: Check 12V support voltage and recent alerts before assuming pack failure
Review service-mode or scan-tool freeze data for temperature and isolation context
Inspect for collision damage, recent coolant loss, or water intrusion history
escalation: Escalate immediately if the vehicle reports isolation or pack-internal faults requiring pack opening or insulation testing.

meaning: Charge-port, pilot/proximity, or onboard charging faults that prevent or interrupt AC charging.
likely_causes: Charge-port latch or proximity signal issue
Pilot communication fault with EVSE
Onboard charger derating due to temperature or supply quality
low_risk_checks: Test with a known-good EVSE before condemning the vehicle
Inspect the charge port for debris, heat damage, or latch problems
Check whether the failure occurs only on AC charging, only on DC fast charging, or both
escalation: Escalate if diagnosis requires charge-port removal, HV charger testing, or service-mode procedures beyond user-safe checks.

meaning: Drive inverter or motor-control fault causing propulsion shutdown, no-drive, or reduced-power events.
likely_causes: Internal drive-unit or inverter fault
Resolver/current-sensor disagreement
Companion thermal derate or low-voltage support issue
low_risk_checks: Check whether the vehicle still enters drive or only enters READY with reduced output
Review companion thermal alerts and 12V support health
Inspect for recent impact damage, coolant loss, or repeated overheating history
escalation: Escalate when confirmation would require drive-unit removal, HV testing, or OEM service-mode procedures beyond safe external checks.

meaning: High-voltage isolation fault detected by the battery or propulsion system.
likely_causes: Moisture intrusion in battery, heater, or A/C-driven thermal components
Damaged orange-cable insulation or connector sealing issue
Internal fault in battery, inverter, compressor, or heater circuits
low_risk_checks: Do not probe HV circuits; start with history of coolant leaks, collision work, or flood exposure
Confirm 12V power and scan all modules for companion thermal or charge faults
Inspect accessible low-voltage connectors, fuses, and coolant loss indicators
escalation: Escalate whenever the next step would require insulation testing or separating HV connectors.

meaning: Battery energy control or charge-enable faults that can block charging or reduce propulsion output.
likely_causes: Battery controller detected a pack, thermal, or contactor concern
Charge session aborted due to temperature or communication fault
Companion faults in charger, coolant pump, or contactor control
low_risk_checks: Identify whether the car refuses AC charging, DC charging, or both
Review battery temperature and coolant-pump activity in live data
Check for software campaign history and recent update or service events
escalation: Escalate if confirmation would require pack contactor testing or HV charger diagnostics.

meaning: Integrated charging control unit or related charging/DC-DC faults affecting 12V support and charging behavior.
likely_causes: ICCU failure or current-sensing issue
12V battery stress caused by DC-DC support loss
Charging interruption due to heat or communication issues
low_risk_checks: Check 12V battery condition and charging-system symptoms first
Determine whether the failure is AC-only, DC-only, or includes 12V warning messages
Inspect charge-port condition, low-voltage fuses, and cooling airflow
escalation: Escalate if ICCU or onboard charger confirmation requires HV component replacement or energized measurements.

meaning: Battery or inverter thermal management issue causing derate, slow charging, or shutdown protection.
likely_causes: Coolant pump, valve, or chiller performance problem
Low coolant level or trapped air after service
Battery preconditioning or HVAC interaction fault
low_risk_checks: Check for recent coolant service, leaks, or repeated fast-charge overheating complaints
Review temperature sensors and coolant flow-related live data
Confirm cabin HVAC and battery conditioning complaints occur together or separately
escalation: Escalate if the next step requires cooling-loop service procedures or HV compressor/inverter testing.

family: Leaf battery temperature / rapid-charge limit families
meaning: Battery temperature or charging-limit condition causing reduced fast-charge performance or power derate.
likely_causes: High pack temperature after repeated fast charging
Temperature sensor disagreement or harness issue
Vehicle operating in hot ambient conditions with limited battery cooling
low_risk_checks: Clarify whether the complaint is slow DC charging after multiple sessions
Review battery temperature bars or scan-tool temperature sensor data
Check for companion charger or 12V support faults
escalation: Escalate if the vehicle reports persistent battery temperature or sensor faults that require pack service.

meaning: Onboard charger or DC-DC converter related fault affecting charge acceptance or 12V system support.
likely_causes: Onboard charger fault
DC-DC converter not maintaining the 12V system
Charge-port communication or EVSE compatibility problem
low_risk_checks: Verify 12V battery health before deeper EV diagnosis
Try a different known-good EVSE and compare AC versus DC charging behavior
Inspect charge-port pins, latch condition, and low-voltage fuses
escalation: Escalate if confirmation would require charger removal, HV connector separation, or energized diagnosis.
